ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for fountain-dispensed post-mix soda; FOODON_03301589 (carbonated beverage) used as the closest broader class.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 is the closest available concept for carbonated soft drinks; no specific fountain-soda concept exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard medium fountain soda served in a cup of approximately 400 ml, as typically dispensed at a fast-food counter in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Carbonated beverage, cola, contains caffeine — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are drawn from USDA SR Legacy entry for regular cola (FDC ID 174851) as the representative fountain soda. The menu at Shake Shack Singapore lists Coca-Cola Original Taste Less Sugar, Coca-Cola Zero Sugar, Sprite, and Fanta Grape; because the blend is variable and the dominant option is a regular-sugar cola, the USDA regular-cola profile is used as the primary reference. Values represent a weighted-average approximation for a standard sugar-containing fountain soda. Zero-sugar variants (Coca-Cola Zero Sugar) would have near-zero energy and carbohydrate; the regular-cola profile is used conservatively to represent the higher-calorie option available.

gi_source.title: Coca-Cola, regular — Glycemic Index Database, University of Sydney (GI = 63)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for regular Coca-Cola, which is the primary sugar-containing variant available at the Shake Shack fountain. This value is used as the representative GI for the regular fountain soda option. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (63 × 42.4) / 100 ≈ 26.7, based on a 400 g serving containing 10.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g (total 42.4 g carbohydrate). Zero-sugar variants (Coca-Cola Zero Sugar) would have GI ≈ 0 and GL ≈ 0; the regular-cola profile is used as the conservative upper bound.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (4 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Fountain soda contains negligible sodium (approximately 16 mg per 400 ml serving), so it does not meaningfully contribute to sodium load in hypertensive individuals; however, the high sugar content may contribute to weight gain and metabolic risk, which are secondary drivers of elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free-sugar content and medium glycemic index (GI 63)
type2_diabetes.guidance: A 400 ml serving of regular fountain soda delivers approximately 42 g of free sugars with a glycemic load of approximately 27, causing a rapid postprandial glucose spike; individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid regular sugar-containing fountain sodas and choose zero-sugar variants or water instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free-sugar intake linked to dyslipidaemia and visceral adiposity
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened fountain sodas is associated with elevated triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, and increased visceral fat accumulation, all of which are independent cardiovascular risk factors; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or eliminate sugar-sweetened beverage intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fountain soda is a carbonated soft drink dispensed at point of service from a post-mix machine that combines pressurised carbonated water with a flavoured syrup concentrate. At Shake Shack Singapore locations (89 Neil Road and One Fullerton), the available options include Coca-Cola Original Taste Less Sugar, Coca-Cola Zero Sugar, Sprite, and Fanta Grape, all served in cups at a price of SGD 4.20. The drink is ultra-processed, contains no protein or fat, and derives virtually all its energy from free sugars (approximately 10.6 g per 100 ml for regular variants). The texture is effervescent and refreshing; flavour profiles range from caramel-cola to citrus-lemon (Sprite) and grape (Fanta). From a clinical nutrition perspective, regular sugar-containing fountain sodas are high-glycaemic beverages (GI ≈ 63 for cola) that deliver a substantial glycaemic load per serving and are strongly associated with increased risk of type 2 diabetes, obesity, and cardiovascular disease when consumed regularly. Zero-sugar variants eliminate the glycaemic concern but may still affect appetite regulation. In Singapore's fast-food context, fountain sodas are a common accompaniment to burger meals and are widely available across Western fast-food chains. Nutritionally, they provide minimal micronutrients and are considered empty-calorie beverages by major dietary guidelines.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are drawn from USDA SR Legacy for regular cola as the closest approved-source match for a fountain soda. The menu includes multiple variants (regular, zero-sugar, Sprite, Fanta Grape) with differing nutritional profiles; the regular-cola profile is used as the representative higher-calorie option. GI is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for Coca-Cola. Confidence is medium rather than high because the record represents a variable multi-variant menu item rather than a single fixed product.
notes: Protein_g is confirmed zero — contains no protein by product formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851). Fat_g is confirmed zero — contains no fat by product formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by formulation. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as carbonated soft drinks contain no dietary fibre. Vitamin values (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, zinc_mg) are reported as 0.0 per USDA SR Legacy — confirmed zero or below detection threshold. Selenium_mcg (0.1) and iron_mg (0.1) are trace amounts per USDA SR Legacy. The menu item encompasses four distinct products: Coca-Cola Original Taste Less Sugar, Coca-Cola Zero Sugar, Sprite, and Fanta Grape. Zero-sugar variants would have energy_kcal ≈ 0 and carbohydrate_g ≈ 0; the regular-cola profile is used as the conservative upper bound for nutritional labelling purposes. GI of 63 applies to regular cola only; zero-sugar variants have effectively zero GI. Glycemic load per serving = (63 × 42.4) / 100 ≈ 26.7 for a 400 g regular-cola serving. This record is classified under Singaporean fast-food context (served at Shake Shack Singapore); multilingual names are provided in Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean cuisine multilingual requirements.
